comment: §6.1 labeled-context BOUNDARY fixtures. Tests the 32-char proximity window. Standard cases use `prefix + gap + body` construction. The `gap` field is the literal string between end of label-separator and start of body (its char length is the distance). Spec: within 32 chars (whitespace/newlines included) after a label. Special cases set `custom_input` for inputs that cannot be expressed as prefix+gap+body.

cases.label: two-labels-29-and-35-REDACT
cases.why: Spec §6.1: first occurrence has gap=35 (> 32, no fire), second occurrence has gap=29 (≤ 32, fires) → overall redact

cases.label: newline-in-gap-REDACT
cases.why: Spec §6.1: newline counts as 1 char toward the 32-char window. gap=1 ≤ 32 → redact.

cases.label: newline-plus-spaces-31-total-REDACT
cases.why: Spec §6.1: newline + 30 spaces = 31 chars total ≤ 32 → redact

cases.label: overlapping-labels-REDACT
cases.why: Spec §6.1: multiple overlapping labels immediately adjacent to the same body → redact
